source_video_title: Jensen Huang: NVIDIA - The $4 Trillion Company & the AI Revolution | Lex Fridman Podcast #499
comment_text: I respectfully disagree. Jensen himself admits that the odds of agents building a company of Nvidia's complexity are 0%. If a system cannot replicate the multi-disciplinary, long-term strategic reasoning required to build a complex hardware-software ecosystem like Nvidia, it suggests we are still in the era of "Narrow" or "Task-Oriented" AI, rather than "General" AI.

source_video_title: David Kirtley: Nuclear Fusion, Plasma Physics, and the Future of Energy | Lex Fridman Podcast #486
comment_text:
I love how David talks about building a bunch of prototypes and that in the long run you make more progress.
That is why SpaceX is so successful.  There first Starship prototype was thought to be a water tower.  Arguments went on for weeks until one day is was clear to everyone it was a rocket.  SpaceX then went about building and blowing up a lot of prototypes and now Starship is finally building block 3 which will be production.     Wish the best to David and his team and keep those prototypes going until you're finally ready to build a working generator that can power a datacenter.  That will be very cool to see.

source_video_title: Deciphering Secrets of Ancient Civilizations, Noah's Ark, and Flood Myths | Lex Fridman Podcast #491
comment_text: 1:03:35 "We're all infantile..." because our culture (especially Western/American culture) does not have mechanisms or expectations that actually move the majority of people to grow into truly Mature human beings. Most of our ideas about "human nature" are based on our observations of immature humans, and thinking, "That's as good as it gets." This becomes more obvious if you have had reason or opportunity to mature beyond that "average" level of human maturity and functioning. Also, this is not just about practical, functional maturing, it's also about spiritual maturing. Christianity tends to encourage a "child-parent" relationship with the Divine and seeking for "unconditional (no expectation) love." In religions that see God as a "guru" or "guruing force" and the individual as a devotee, not just a believer, there are far more demands for self-awareness, self-discipline, and growth, or higher and higher levels of realization or understanding, including practical and spiritual maturing.

source_video_title: Jason Fried: Your Only Competition Is Your Costs
comment_text:
This is an incredible interviewee and interviewer. I know that's such a corny thing that boomers say on YouTube comments, but I actually feel it's true here. 

The book Rework really changed my life when I read it 15 years ago. A lot of the lessons in there are still extremely relevant. (Admittedly, some I disagree with.)

Having read that book so many times, I think I've mentally undersold how much of Fried's personality I've subtly ingested into my own.
